sections.paragraph:
σχηματίζω aor. ἐσχημάτισα (s. prec. entry; Hippocr., Pla., POxy 2267, 13 [IVa.d.], Philo, Just.—Gener. ‘give form’ to someth., hence ‘make ready’ for someth.) to cause to be in an appropriate position for someth., position (on the freq. use of ς. in ref. to posture s. L-S-J-M) ἐσχημάτισεν αὐτήν (Salome) positioned (Mary) for a probe of her virginity GJs 20:1 pap; difft. codd.: σχημάτισον σαυτήν put yourself into position (spoken by the midwife), followed by Mary’s action ἐσχημάτισεν αὑτήν (for Salome’s examination).—DELG s.v. ἔχω.

aor. aor. = aorist

prec. prec. = preceding

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

ref. ref. = reference(s)

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

difft. difft. = different(ly)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 981
